title: Community meal ration board
summary: A transparent meal board for fuel limits, vulnerable needs, allergies, fair serving, and leftover safety.
content: Shared meals can stabilize a group or create resentment and illness. The board names what expires first, what fuel exists, who needs priority, what restrictions matter, and how leftovers will be handled. Fairness is not always identical portions; it is clear reasons and a review time.
steps: List vulnerable needs privately but clearly for cooks.
warnings: Do not hide allergens to avoid arguments.
Do not stretch meals with unsafe water or spoiled food.
Do not cook indoors with unsafe ventilation.
common_mistakes: Letting the loudest person control serving.
Cooking high-fuel meals when fuel is scarce.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Community meal ration board with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Community meal ration board if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Community meal ration board and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Community meal ration board or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Community meal ration board are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Community meal ration board”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Community meal ration board as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Community meal ration board when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Community meal ration board.
short_term: For the first hours of Community meal ration board, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Community meal ration board remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Community meal ration board fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Community meal ration board to the real environment: check water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
